# docsfy

> AI-powered documentation generator that creates polished, Mintlify-quality static HTML sites from GitHub repositories.

## Getting Started

- [Introduction](introduction.md): Overview of docsfy, what it does, why it exists, and how it uses AI CLI tools to generate professional documentation from GitHub repositories.
- [Quickstart](quickstart.md): Get docsfy running in under 5 minutes: install prerequisites, configure an AI provider, and generate your first documentation site.
- [Installation](installation.md): Detailed installation instructions covering Docker deployment, local development setup, Python and uv requirements, and AI CLI binary installation.

## Configuration

- [Environment Variables](environment-variables.md): Complete reference for all environment variables including AI_PROVIDER, AI_MODEL, AI_CLI_TIMEOUT, API keys, and logging configuration.
- [AI Providers](ai-providers.md): How to configure and switch between supported AI providers: Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Cursor Agent, including authentication setup for each.
- [Docker Deployment](docker-deployment.md): Docker and Docker Compose configuration, volume mounts for data persistence and credentials, health checks, and production deployment guidance.

## Usage

- [Generating Documentation](generating-docs.md): How to trigger documentation generation for a GitHub repository, monitor progress, and access the finished site via the API or browser.
- [Incremental Updates](incremental-updates.md): How docsfy tracks repository commits and regenerates only affected pages, reducing generation time and AI usage on subsequent runs.
- [Downloading & Self-Hosting](downloading-and-self-hosting.md): How to download generated documentation as a tar.gz archive and deploy the static HTML site to your own hosting provider.
- [Managing Projects](managing-projects.md): Listing, inspecting, and deleting documentation projects through the API, including status tracking and generation history.

## Architecture

- [Generation Pipeline](generation-pipeline.md): Deep dive into the four-stage pipeline: repository cloning, AI-driven planning, concurrent content generation, and HTML rendering with Jinja2.
- [Storage & Database](storage-and-database.md): How docsfy stores data using SQLite for project metadata and the filesystem for cached markdown, plan files, and rendered HTML sites.
- [HTML Theming & Output](html-theming.md): Overview of the generated HTML site features: sidebar navigation, dark/light theme toggle, client-side search, syntax highlighting, callout boxes, and responsive design.

## API Reference

- [API Overview](api-overview.md): Introduction to the docsfy REST API, base URL, request/response formats, and authentication requirements.
- [POST /api/generate](api-generate.md): Start documentation generation for a GitHub repository URL, including request body schema, response format, and error handling.
- [GET /api/status](api-status.md): List all tracked projects with their current generation status, last generated timestamp, and commit SHA.
- [Project Endpoints](api-projects.md): GET, DELETE, and download endpoints for individual projects: retrieve details, remove projects, and export sites as tar.gz archives.
- [GET /docs/{project}/{path}](api-docs-serving.md): How docsfy serves generated HTML documentation directly, including URL routing and path resolution.
- [GET /health](api-health.md): Health check endpoint used for container orchestration, monitoring, and load balancer readiness probes.

## Development

- [Contributing](contributing.md): Development workflow, code style conventions, pre-commit hooks (ruff, mypy, flake8, gitleaks), and how to run the test suite with tox.
- [Project Structure](project-structure.md): Directory layout, module organization, key source files, and how the FastAPI application, AI provider integrations, and rendering engine fit together.
